title: Index
summary: Defines triples (a,b,c) of Laplacian exponents with a+b+c=N and the elementary moves between neighboring terms.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Defines triples (a,b,c) of Laplacian exponents with a+b+c=N and the elementary moves between neighboring terms.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows PolyGJMSoperators/OR/Index.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The coefficient index (a,b,c) of total degree N. Its mathematical data are: a: the natural numbers; b: the natural numbers; c: the natural numbers; sum eq: a plus b plus c equals N.
The rank-two Laplacian word encoded by an OR index. In explicit terms, for n in the natural numbers, and i of type orindex n, its defining expression is Bi Laplacian Word.mk I.a I.b I.c.
correspondence.leanType: def toWord {N : ℕ} (I : ORIndex N) : LaplacianWord 2

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For N in the natural numbers, and I of type ORIndex N, I.to Word.total Power equals N.
correspondence.leanType: theorem toWord_totalPower {N : ℕ} (I : ORIndex N) : I.toWord.totalPower = N

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Raw finite support for all triples with entries bounded by N. In explicit terms, for n in the natural numbers, its defining expression is Finset.range N plus 1.product Finset.range N plus 1.product Finset.range N plus 1.filter the function sending p to p.1.1 plus p.1.2 plus p.2 equals N.
correspondence.leanType: def supportRaw (N : ℕ) : Finset ((ℕ × ℕ) × ℕ)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The canonical finite OR support of all coefficient indices of degree N.
For N in the natural numbers, I of type ORIndex N, and hI of type I belongs to support N, I.to Word.total Power equals N.
correspondence.leanType: theorem support_toWord_totalPower {N : ℕ} {I : ORIndex N} (_hI : I ∈ support N) : I.toWord.totalPower = N

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Move one unit from the output Laplacian power to the first input power. In explicit terms, for n in the natural numbers, i of type orindex n, and ha of type 0 < i.a, its defining expression is a : equals I.a minus 1 b : equals I.b plus 1 c : equals I.c sum eq : equals by have hsum : equals I.sum eq omega.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \operatorname{move\_a\_to\_b}(I,ha)=a = a_{\mathrm{I}} - 1 b = b_{\mathrm{I}} + 1 c = c_{\mathrm{I}} \operatorname{sum\_eq} = \operatorname{by} \operatorname{have} \operatorname{hsum} = I.\operatorname{sum\_eq} \operatorname{omega}
correspondence.leanType: def move_a_to_b {N : ℕ} (I : ORIndex N) (ha : 0 < I.a) : ORIndex N

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Move one unit from the output Laplacian power to the second input power. In explicit terms, for n in the natural numbers, i of type orindex n, and ha of type 0 < i.a, its defining expression is a : equals I.a minus 1 b : equals I.b c : equals I.c plus 1 sum eq : equals by have hsum : equals I.sum eq omega.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \operatorname{move\_a\_to\_c}(I,ha)=a = a_{\mathrm{I}} - 1 b = b_{\mathrm{I}} c = c_{\mathrm{I}} + 1 \operatorname{sum\_eq} = \operatorname{by} \operatorname{have} \operatorname{hsum} = I.\operatorname{sum\_eq} \operatorname{omega}
correspondence.leanType: def move_a_to_c {N : ℕ} (I : ORIndex N) (ha : 0 < I.a) : ORIndex N

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Move one unit from the first input power to the output Laplacian power. In explicit terms, for n in the natural numbers, i of type orindex n, and hb of type 0 < i.b, its defining expression is a : equals I.a plus 1 b : equals I.b minus 1 c : equals I.c sum eq : equals by have hsum : equals I.sum eq omega.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \operatorname{move\_b\_to\_a}(I,hb)=a = a_{\mathrm{I}} + 1 b = b_{\mathrm{I}} - 1 c = c_{\mathrm{I}} \operatorname{sum\_eq} = \operatorname{by} \operatorname{have} \operatorname{hsum} = I.\operatorname{sum\_eq} \operatorname{omega}
correspondence.leanType: def move_b_to_a {N : ℕ} (I : ORIndex N) (hb : 0 < I.b) : ORIndex N

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Move one unit from the second input power to the output Laplacian power. In explicit terms, for n in the natural numbers, i of type orindex n, and hc of type 0 < i.c, its defining expression is a : equals I.a plus 1 b : equals I.b c : equals I.c minus 1 sum eq : equals by have hsum : equals I.sum eq omega.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \operatorname{move\_c\_to\_a}(I,hc)=a = a_{\mathrm{I}} + 1 b = b_{\mathrm{I}} c = c_{\mathrm{I}} - 1 \operatorname{sum\_eq} = \operatorname{by} \operatorname{have} \operatorname{hsum} = I.\operatorname{sum\_eq} \operatorname{omega}
correspondence.leanType: def move_c_to_a {N : ℕ} (I : ORIndex N) (hc : 0 < I.c) : ORIndex N

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Move one unit from the first input power to the second input power. In explicit terms, for n in the natural numbers, i of type orindex n, and hb of type 0 < i.b, its defining expression is a : equals I.a b : equals I.b minus 1 c : equals I.c plus 1 sum eq : equals by have hsum : equals I.sum eq omega.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \operatorname{move\_b\_to\_c}(I,hb)=a = a_{\mathrm{I}} b = b_{\mathrm{I}} - 1 c = c_{\mathrm{I}} + 1 \operatorname{sum\_eq} = \operatorname{by} \operatorname{have} \operatorname{hsum} = I.\operatorname{sum\_eq} \operatorname{omega}
correspondence.leanType: def move_b_to_c {N : ℕ} (I : ORIndex N) (hb : 0 < I.b) : ORIndex N

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Move one unit from the second input power to the first input power. In explicit terms, for n in the natural numbers, i of type orindex n, and hc of type 0 < i.c, its defining expression is a : equals I.a b : equals I.b plus 1 c : equals I.c minus 1 sum eq : equals by have hsum : equals I.sum eq omega.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \operatorname{move\_c\_to\_b}(I,hc)=a = a_{\mathrm{I}} b = b_{\mathrm{I}} + 1 c = c_{\mathrm{I}} - 1 \operatorname{sum\_eq} = \operatorname{by} \operatorname{have} \operatorname{hsum} = I.\operatorname{sum\_eq} \operatorname{omega}
correspondence.leanType: def move_c_to_b {N : ℕ} (I : ORIndex N) (hc : 0 < I.c) : ORIndex N

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For N in the natural numbers, I of type ORIndex N, and ha of type 0 < I.a, 0 < N minus I.b.
correspondence.leanType: theorem N_sub_b_pos_of_a_pos {N : ℕ} (I : ORIndex N) (ha : 0 < I.a) : 0 < N - I.b

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For N in the natural numbers, I of type ORIndex N, and ha of type 0 < I.a, 0 < N minus I.c.
correspondence.leanType: theorem N_sub_c_pos_of_a_pos {N : ℕ} (I : ORIndex N) (ha : 0 < I.a) : 0 < N - I.c

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For N in the natural numbers, I of type ORIndex N, and ha of type 0 < I.a, N minus I.b minus 1 equals N minus I.b plus 1.
correspondence.leanType: theorem N_sub_b_pred_eq {N : ℕ} (I : ORIndex N) (_ha : 0 < I.a) : N - I.b - 1 = N - (I.b + 1)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For N in the natural numbers, I of type ORIndex N, and ha of type 0 < I.a, N minus I.c minus 1 equals N minus I.c plus 1.
correspondence.leanType: theorem N_sub_c_pred_eq {N : ℕ} (I : ORIndex N) (_ha : 0 < I.a) : N - I.c - 1 = N - (I.c + 1)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For N in the natural numbers, and I of type ORIndex N, I.b plus 1 plus I.c equals I.b plus I.c plus 1.
correspondence.leanType: theorem b_succ_add_c {N : ℕ} (I : ORIndex N) : I.b + 1 + I.c = I.b + I.c + 1

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For N in the natural numbers, and I of type ORIndex N, I.b plus I.c plus 1 equals I.b plus I.c plus 1.
correspondence.leanType: theorem b_add_c_succ {N : ℕ} (I : ORIndex N) : I.b + (I.c + 1) = I.b + I.c + 1

references.locator: Classification of invariant bilinear differential operators
references.note: Flat-model origin of the coefficient family.
Primary curved ambient source for the encoded OR recurrence.

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
